description: Figure 3 presents SEM micrographs and EPMA elemental mapping of Fe–30Mn–8Al–C low-density steels with 1 wt% Cu (a) and 3 wt% Cu (b). The secondary electron images reveal white particles distributed on the austenite matrix surface and along grain boundaries. Corresponding EPMA maps demonstrate that in the 1Cu sample, Al and Mo are uniformly distributed throughout the austenite matrix, while Nb and C co-localize precisely at the white particle positions (highlighted by red boxes), identifying them as NbC-type carbides. Slight Cu enrichment is observed along austenite grain boundaries. In contrast, the 3Cu sample exhibits strong Cu segregation along grain boundaries with uneven Al distribution at specific boundaries. The elemental mapping confirms that some white particles on the surface and at grain boundaries are elemental Cu particles, while remaining particles are NbC carbides. These observations reveal that increasing Cu content from 1% to 3% promotes Cu precipitation at grain boundaries and alters carbide distribution, providing microstructural evidence for understanding Cu's role in corrosion behavior.
